title: Methods and compositions for the treatment of vascular anomalies
abstract: The technology described herein is directed to methods and compositions relating to the treatment of vascaular anomalies, e.g. hemangiomas, including the discovery that famesyltransferase and/or geranylgeranyltransferas inhibitors target pathways controlling the differentiation of hemangioma-derived stem cells.
claims:
Claims What is claimed herein is: 1. A method of treating a vascular anomaly in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering a composition comprising at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor to the subject. 2. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the famesyltransferase inhibitor is selected from the group consisting of: tipifamib; lonafamib; LNK-754; chaetomellic acid; clarvaric acid; FPT Inhibitor I (sc- 221625); FPT Inhibitor II (sc-221626); FPT Inhibitor III (sc-221627); FTase Inhibitor I (sc-221632); FTase Inhibitor II (sc-221633), FTI-276; FTI-277; FGTI-2734; GGTI- 297; L-744,832; manumycin A; gingerol; gliotoxin; and alpha-hydrozy famesyl phosphonic acid. 3. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the famesyltransferase inhibitor is tipifamib. 4. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the famesyltransferase inhibitor is lonfamib. 5. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the geranylgeranyltransferase inhibitor is selected from the group consisting of: GGTI-2418; and FGTI-2734. 6. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the vascular anomaly is selected from the group consisting of: infantile hemangioma, congenital hemangioma, pyogenic granuloma, tufted angioma, kaposiform hemangioendothelioma, epitheloid hemangioendothelioma (EHE), capillary malformations, arteriovenous malformations, venous malformations, lymphatic malformations, complex lymphatic malformations, generalized lymphatic anomaly, central conducting lymphatic anomaly, kaposiform lymphangiomatosis, and Gorham-Stout disease. 7. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the vascular anomaly is infantile hemangioma. 8. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is further administered at least one statin, beta-blocker, mTOR inhibitor, and/or steroid. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the statin is selected from the group consisting of: simvastatin; atorvastatin; cerivastatin; fluvastatin; lovastatin; mevastatin; pitavastatin; pravastatin; and rosuvastatin. 10. The method of claim 7, wherein the statin is simvastatin. 11. The method of claim 7, wherein the statin is atorvastatin. 12. The method of claim 7, wherein the beta-blocker is selected from the group consisting of: R+ atenolol; atenolol; nadolol; propranolol; R+propranolol; and timolol. 13. The method of claim 7, wherein the beta-blocker is selected from the group consisting of: R+ atenolol; atenolol; propranolol; and R+propranolol. 14. The method of claim 7, wherein the beta-blocker is R+ propranolol. 15. The method of claim 7, wherein the mTOR inhibitor is selected from the group consisting of: sirolimus; everolimus; temisrolimus; and rapamycin. 16. The method of claim 7, wherein the mTOR inhibitor is rapamycin. 17. The method of claim 7, wherein the steroid is a corticosteroid. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the corticosteroid is selected from the group consisting of: dexamethasone; prednisone; prednisolone; triamcinolone; clobetasol propionate; betamethasone valerate; betamethasone dipropionate; and mometasone furoate. 19. The method of any of the preceeding claims, wherein the composition comprising at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of between 0.00001 mg/kg/d and 5000 mg/kg/d. 20. The method of any of the preceeding claims, wherein the composition comprising at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of between 0.0001 mg/kg/d and 500 mg/kg/d. 21. The method of any of the preceeding claims, wherein the composition comprising at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of between 0.001 mg/kg/d and 50 mg/kg/d. 22. The method of any of the preceeding claims, wherein the composition comprising at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of between 0.001 mg/kg/d and 10 mg/kg/d. 23. The method of any of the preceeding claims, wherein the composition comprising at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of 10 mg/kg/d or higher. 24. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is a mammal. 25. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the subject is a human. 26. A combination or kit comprising: at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyltransferase inhibitor; and at least one statin; beta-blocker, mTOR inhibitor, and/or steroid. 27. A combination or kit comprising: 

 at least one farnesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyltransferase inhibitor; and at least one statin, beta-blocker, mTOR inhibitor, and/or steroid; for use in treating a vascular anomaly. 28. A composition comprising at least one farnesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyltransferase inhibitor, for use in treating a vascular anomaly. 29. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the farnesyltransferase inhibitor is selected from the group consisting of: tipifamib; lonafamib; LNK-754; chaetomellic acid; clarvaric acid; FPT Inhibitor I (sc- 221625); FPT Inhibitor II (sc-221626); FPT Inhibitor III (sc-221627); FTase Inhibitor I (sc-221632); FTase Inhibitor II (sc-221633), FTI-276; FTI-277; FGTI-2734; GGTI- 297; L-744,832; manumycin A; gingerol; gliotoxin; and alpha-hydrozy famesyl phosphonic acid. 30. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the farnesyltransferase inhibitor is tipifamib. 31. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the farnesyltransferase inhibitor is lonfamib. 32. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the geranylgeranyltransferase inhibitor is selected from the group consisting of: GGTI-2418; and FGTI-2734. 33. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the statin is selected from the group consisting of: simvastatin; atorvastatin; cerivastatin; fluvastatin; lovastatin; mevastatin; pitavastatin; pravastatin; and rosuvastatin. 34. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the statin is simvastatin. 35. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the statin is atorvastatin. 36. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the betablocker is selected from the group consisting of: R+ atenolol; atenolol; nadolol; propranolol; R+propranolol; and timolol. 37. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the betablocker is selected from the group consisting of: R+ atenolol; atenolol; propranolol; and R+propranolol. 38. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein beta-blocker is R+ propranolol. 39. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the mTOR inhibitor is selected from the group consisting of: sirolimus; everolimus; temisrolimus; and rapamycin. 40. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the mTOR inhibitor is rapamycin. 41. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the steroid is a corticosteroid. 42. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the corticosteroid is selected from the group consisting of: dexamethasone; prednisone; prednisolone; triamcinolone; clobetasol propionate; betamethasone valerate; betamethasone dipropionate; and mometasone furoate. 43. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims is formulated for oral administration. 44. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims is a syrup or suspension. 45. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the vascular anomaly is selected from the group consisting of: infantile hemangioma, congenital hemangioma, pyogenic granuloma, tufted angioma, kaposiform hemangioendothelioma, epitheloid hemangioendothelioma (EHE), capillary malformations, arteriovenous malformations, venous malformations, lymphatic malformations, complex lymphatic malformations, generalized lymphatic anomaly, central conducting lymphatic anomaly, kaposiform lymphangiomatosis, and Gorham-Stout disease. 46. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the vascular anomaly is infantile hemangioma. 47. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition comprising at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of between 0.00001 mg/kg/d and 5000 mg/kg/d. 48. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition comprising at least one famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of between 0.0001 mg/kg/d and 500 mg/kg/d. 49. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition comprising famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of between 0.001 mg/kg/d and 50 mg/kg/d. 50. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition comprising famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of between 0.001 mg/kg/d and 10 mg/kg/d. 51. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition comprising famesyltransferase inhibitor and/or at least one geranylgeranyl transferase inhibitor is administered to the subject at a dose of 10 mg/kg/d or higher. 52. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition is administered to a subject, wherein the subject is a mammal. 53. The combination, kit, or composition of one of the preceding claims, wherein the combination, kit, or composition is administered to a subject, wherein the subject is a human.
